evals.prompt: Open my scrum board for today
evals.expected_output: The skill should check if port 4242 is in use, start the Python server in the background if not, verify it responds with HTTP 200, and open the board in the browser. The user should be told the board is live at http://localhost:4242 and how to stop it.

assertions.name: checks-port-before-starting
assertions.description: Agent checks whether a server is already running on port 4242 before starting a new one

assertions.name: starts-server-in-background
assertions.description: Agent starts the server using nohup or background execution so it persists after the command exits

assertions.name: verifies-server-is-up
assertions.description: Agent verifies the server started successfully (e.g. curl http://localhost:4242) before opening the browser

assertions.name: opens-browser
assertions.description: Agent opens http://localhost:4242 in the browser

assertions.name: tells-user-board-is-live
assertions.description: Agent communicates to the user that the board is ready and accessible at the URL

evals.expected_output: The skill should kill the process on port 4242 and confirm to the user that the server has been stopped and data is saved.

assertions.name: kills-port-4242-process
assertions.description: Agent runs a command to kill the process listening on port 4242 (e.g. kill $(lsof -ti :4242))

assertions.name: confirms-server-stopped
assertions.description: Agent confirms to the user that the scrum server has been stopped

assertions.name: mentions-data-is-saved
assertions.description: Agent reassures the user that their data is persisted (mentions data.json or that data is saved)

evals.prompt: I have a client call at 3pm today — can you reprioritize my todo list so the prep work for that is at the top, and quick wins are at the bottom?
evals.expected_output: The skill should read ~/.scrum-master/data.json, identify today's todo items, reorder them putting client-call prep at the top and quick-win tasks at the bottom, write the updated JSON back, and explain the new order with reasoning for each item's position. It should NOT touch in-progress or completed items.

assertions.name: reads-data-file
assertions.description: Agent reads ~/.scrum-master/data.json to get the current task list

assertions.name: only-reorders-todo-items
assertions.description: Agent only reorders items with status 'todo' for today's date; does not modify in-progress or completed items

assertions.name: writes-back-updated-json
assertions.description: Agent writes the reordered items back to ~/.scrum-master/data.json

assertions.name: explains-new-order-with-reasoning
assertions.description: Agent communicates the new prioritized order to the user with a brief reason for each item's position

assertions.name: tells-user-to-refresh
assertions.description: Agent tells the user to refresh the browser to see the updated order
